title: Scheduling of idle mode activities in mobile devices
abstract: This disclosure includes example methods for scheduling idle mode activities on a multiple subscriber identity module (SIM), multiple standby mobile communication device configured for communications via a first subscription and a second subscription. Various examples may include scheduling, by a processor of the mobile communication device, a first paging indicator channel (PICH) reading or a first system information block (SIB) reading on the first subscription, scheduling, by the processor, a first idle mode measurement on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription, and performing, by the processor, the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the first idle mode measurement on the second subscription.
claims:
Claims THE CLAIMS 1. A method for scheduling idle mode activities on a mobile communication device configured for communications via a first subscription and a second subscription, comprising: scheduling, by a processor of the mobile communication device, a first paging indicator channel (PICH) reading or a first system information block (SIB) reading on the first subscription; scheduling, by the processor, a first idle mode measurement on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription; and performing the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the first idle mode measurement on the second subscription. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the mobile communication device comprises a radio frequency (RF) resource including a first receive chain and a second receive chain. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein: the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription is scheduled on the first receive chain; and the first idle mode measurement on the second subscription is scheduled on the second receive chain. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the first idle mode measurement comprises one of an intra- frequency measurement, an inter-frequency measurement, and an inter-radio access technology (RAT) measurement. 5. The method of claim 1, further comprising: 

 scheduling, by the processor, a second PICH reading or a second SIB reading on the second subscription; scheduling, by the processor, a second idle mode measurement on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the second PICH reading or the second SIB reading on the second subscription; and performing the second PICH reading or the second SIB reading on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the second idle mode measurement on the first subscription. 6. The method of claim 1, further comprising: scheduling, by the processor, a third PICH reading or a third SIB reading on the first subscription; scheduling, by the processor, a discontinuous reception (DRX) or a discontinuous transmission (DTX) wake-up on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the third PICH reading or the third SIB reading on the first subscription; and performing the third PICH reading or the third SIB reading on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the DRX or DTX wake-up on the second subscription. 7. A mobile communication device, comprising: a processor configured to support a first subscription and second subscription and configured to: schedule a first paging indicator channel (PICH) reading or a first system information block (SIB) reading on the first subscription; schedule a first idle mode measurement on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription; and 

 perform the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the first idle mode measurement on the second subscription. 8. The mobile communication device of claim 7, further comprising a radio frequency (RF) resource including a first receive chain and a second receive chain. 9. The mobile communication device of claim 8, wherein: the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription is scheduled on the first receive chain; and the first idle mode measurement on the second subscription is scheduled on the second receive chain. 10. The mobile communication device of claim 7, wherein the first idle mode measurement comprises one of an intra-frequency measurement, an inter- frequency measurement, and an inter-radio access technology (RAT) measurement. 11. The mobile communication device of claim 7, wherein the processor is further configured to: schedule a second PICH reading or a second SIB reading on the second subscription; schedule a second idle mode measurement on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the second PICH reading or the second SIB reading on the second subscription; and perform the second PICH reading or the second SIB reading on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the second idle mode measurement on the first subscription. 12. The mobile communication device of claim 7, wherein the processor is further configured to: schedule a third PICH reading or a third SIB reading on the first subscription; schedule a discontinuous reception (DRX) or a discontinuous transmission (DTX) wake-up on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the third PICH reading or the third SIB reading on the first subscription; and perform the third PICH reading or the third SIB reading on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the DRX or DTX wake-up on the second subscription. 13. A mobile communication device configured for communications via a first subscription and a second subscription, comprising: means for scheduling a first paging indicator channel (PICH) reading or a first system information block (SIB) reading on the first subscription; means for scheduling a first idle mode measurement on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription; and means for performing the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the first idle mode measurement on the second subscription. 14. The mobile communication device of claim 13, wherein the mobile communication device comprises a radio frequency (RF) resource including a first receive chain and a second receive chain. 15. The mobile communication device of claim 14, wherein: the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription is scheduled on the first receive chain; and 

 the first idle mode measurement on the second subscription is scheduled on the second receive chain. 16. The mobile communication device of claim 13, wherein the first idle mode measurement comprises one of an intra-frequency measurement, an inter- frequency measurement, and an inter-radio access technology (RAT) measurement. 17. The mobile communication device of claim 13, further comprising: means for scheduling a second PICH reading or a second SIB reading on the second subscription; means for scheduling a second idle mode measurement on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the second PICH reading or the second SIB reading on the second subscription; and means for performing the second PICH reading or the second SIB reading on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the second idle mode measurement on the first subscription. 18. The mobile communication device of claim 13, further comprising: means for scheduling a third PICH reading or a third SIB reading on the first subscription; means for scheduling a discontinuous reception (DRX) or a discontinuous transmission (DTX) wake-up on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the third PICH reading or the third SIB reading on the first subscription; and means for performing the third PICH reading or the third SIB reading on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the DRX or DTX wake-up on the second subscription. 19. A non-transitory processor readable storage medium having stored thereon processor-executable instructions configured to cause a processor of a mobile communication device to perform operations comprising: scheduling a first paging indicator channel (PICH) reading or a first system information block (SIB) reading on a first subscription; scheduling a first idle mode measurement on a second subscription at a time that overlaps with the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription; and performing the first PICH reading or the first SIB reading on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the first idle mode measurement on the second subscription. 20. The non-transitory processor readable storage medium of claim 19, wherein the first idle mode measurement comprises one of an intra-frequency measurement, an inter- frequency measurement, and an inter-radio access technology (RAT) measurement. 21. The non-transitory processor readable storage medium of claim 19, wherein the stored processor-executable instructions are configured to cause the processor of a mobile communication device to perform operations further comprising: scheduling a second PICH reading or a second SIB reading on the second subscription; scheduling a second idle mode measurement on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the second PICH reading or the second SIB reading on the second subscription; and performing the second PICH reading or the second SIB reading on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the second idle mode measurement on the first subscription. 22. The non-transitory processor readable storage medium of claim 19, wherein the stored processor-executable instructions are configured to cause the processor of a mobile communication device to perform operations further comprising: scheduling a third PICH reading or a third SIB reading on the first subscription; scheduling a discontinuous reception (DRX) or a discontinuous transmission (DTX) wake-up on the second subscription at a time that overlaps with the third PICH reading or the third SIB reading on the first subscription; and performing the third PICH reading or the third SIB reading on the first subscription at a time that overlaps with the DRX or DTX wake-up on the second subscription.
